member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.CoerceValueHelper.Coerce(System.Object,System.Func{System.String,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.CellValue},System.Func{System.DateTimeOffset,System.DateTime})
member.summary: Converts an arbitrary CLR value into the and tuple used by Excel.
member.param: The value to be represented in a worksheet cell.
Delegate that materialises a entry for shared strings.

Optional delegate used to convert instances into values before calculating the Excel serial number. When omitted, is used. The provided strategy only influences the numeric serial value that is written; cell formatting must still be applied separately.
member.returns: The OpenXML representation of the supplied value.
member.remarks: Integer values with an absolute magnitude above ±9,007,199,254,740,992 are written using their string form to prevent double precision loss while keeping the cell typed as .
member.exception: Thrown when the resulting shared-string payload exceeds Excel's 32,767 character limit.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument
member.summary: Represents an Excel document and provides methods for creating, loading and saving spreadsheets.
Indexer helpers for convenient sheet access (name or 0-based index).
Convenience bridge methods exposing read helpers on an open ExcelDocument to avoid re-opening files.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.SheetCachingEnabled
member.summary: Enables caching of wrappers for faster repeat access at the cost of higher memory usage. Set to to avoid holding references to every sheet in very large workbooks.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.TableNameComparer
member.summary: Controls how workbook-level table name uniqueness is compared. Defaults to . Changing this will reset the internal cache and rebuild it on next use. Set it once before adding tables for predictable behavior.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.DefaultChartStylePreset
member.summary: Optional default chart style preset applied to charts created in this workbook.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.Execution
member.summary: Execution policy for controlling parallel vs sequential operations.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.DateTimeOffsetWriteStrategy
member.summary: Controls how values are converted to before being written to worksheet cells. Defaults to .
member.remarks: The delegate influences the numeric serial value stored in the cell but does not automatically change number formats. Apply the desired cell formatting separately.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.SetNamedRange(System.String,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,OfficeIMO.Excel.NameValidationMode)
member.summary: Creates or updates a defined name pointing to an A1 range. When is provided, the name is local to that sheet; otherwise it is workbook‑global.
member.param: Defined name to create or update.
A1 range (e.g. "A1:B10"). Can include a sheet prefix.
Optional sheet scope for a local name.
When true, saves the workbook after the change.
When true, marks the defined name as hidden.
Controls how the name and range are validated: Sanitize (default) clamps/adjusts; Strict throws on invalid input.

member.returns: True if the name existed and was removed; otherwise false.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.SetPrintTitles(OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets rows/columns to repeat at top/left when printing a specific sheet by creating a sheet-local defined name _xlnm.Print_Titles. Pass nulls to clear existing print titles.
member.param: First row to repeat (1-based), or null.
Last row to repeat (1-based), or null.
First column to repeat (1-based), or null.
Last column to repeat (1-based), or null.
Whether to save the workbook after the change.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.RepairDefinedNames(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Repairs common issues with defined names that can trigger Excel's file repair, such as duplicates within the same scope, invalid LocalSheetId after sheet reordering/removal, or references containing #REF!.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.NormalizeRange(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes an A1-style range, ensuring absolute references and validating format. Accepts an optional sheet prefix (e.g. ''Sheet1'!A1:B2'). Throws if the input is not a valid A1 range or cell reference.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.EnsureValidDefinedName(System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.NameValidationMode)
member.summary: Ensures a defined name complies with Excel rules. In Sanitize mode, returns a corrected name. Throws in Strict mode when input is invalid. Rules: - 1..255 characters - First char must be a letter or underscore - Allowed characters: letters, digits, underscore, period - Cannot look like a cell reference (e.g., A1, AA10) or an R1C1 reference - Cannot be TRUE or FALSE (case-insensitive)

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.GetPivotTables
member.summary: Returns all pivot tables defined in the workbook.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.CreateReader(OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Creates a reader that shares this document's underlying OpenXML handle (no new file handle). Caller should dispose the reader after use; it will not close this document.

member.summary: Starts a fluent read pipeline over this open document.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument.GetTables

Also list defined names in a dedicated column.
When listing defined names, include ones marked Hidden.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSaveOptions
member.summary: Optional behaviors applied during and to strengthen robustness and CI validation.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSaveOptions.SafeRepairDefinedNames
member.summary: When true, attempts to repair common defined-name issues (duplicates, out-of-range LocalSheetId, #REF!) before save.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSaveOptions.ValidateOpenXml
member.summary: When true, validates the saved package using OpenXmlValidator and throws on any errors.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSaveOptions.SafePreflight
member.summary: When true, performs a safety preflight on all worksheets before saving, removing empty containers (e.g., empty Hyperlinks/MergeCells), dropping orphaned drawing/header-footer references, and cleaning up invalid table references. This can prevent rare "Repaired Records" notices in Excel.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSaveOptions.None
member.summary: Returns an options instance with all features disabled.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet
member.summary: Helpers for worksheet cell comments (notes).
Represents a single worksheet within an .
Data operations: autofilter, sorting (values-only), find/replace, and validation. Kept small and focused; advanced formatting lives in dedicated files.
Header-based helpers for addressing cells and columns by header name.
Header-based style helpers.
Helpers for inserting images anchored to worksheet cells.
Page setup helpers for orientation and margins.
Read convenience methods exposed directly on ExcelSheet to avoid separate reader usage.
Helpers for adding sparklines to worksheets.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.UpdateSheetDimension

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.EnsureWorksheetElementOrder
member.summary: Ensures all worksheet elements are in the correct order according to OpenXML schema. This must be called before saving to prevent validation errors.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.ExecuteWithPolicy(System.String,System.Int32,System.Nullable{OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionMode},System.Action,System.Action,System.Action,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Core execution helper that handles a compute/apply split with optional parallelization. Compute runs without locks; only the apply stage is serialized under a document-level lock.
member.remarks: Pattern guidance: - The delegate must not touch the OpenXML DOM or any shared mutable state. It should prepare immutable data structures for the apply stage. - The delegate runs once under a writer lock and may mutate the DOM. - Prefer coarse-grained apply operations (e.g., single Append with a buffer) to minimize DOM churn. - Use to provide a best-effort scale hint; it need not be exact.
member.param: A short operation name used for diagnostics and policy decisions.
Approximate number of items to process; used to decide parallel vs sequential. This does not have to be exact but should reflect relative workload.
Force a specific execution mode (Sequential/Parallel); null to use policy (Automatic).
Single-threaded path. Used either when policy decides sequential or when compute/apply are not provided.
Compute phase that is safe to run without locks and must not mutate the OpenXML DOM.
Apply phase that writes to the DOM. This runs once under a serialized lock.
Cancellation token for the compute phase.

member.summary: Adds an image anchored to the specified cell. The top-left of the image will align to the cell's top-left, with optional pixel offsets. Size is specified in pixels and converted to EMUs.
member.param: Content type, e.g. image/png or image/jpeg.
Optional X offset from cell origin in pixels.
Optional Y offset from cell origin in pixels.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.AddImageFromUrlAt(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Downloads an image from URL (with timeout and size limits) and anchors it to the specified cell.
member.param: 1-based row index where the top edge of the image will be anchored.
1-based column index where the left edge of the image will be anchored.
Remote image URL to download. Requests timeout after 5 seconds and must be smaller than 2 MB.
Desired image width in pixels. Defaults to 96 px, converted to English Metric Units (EMUs) for OpenXML positioning.
Desired image height in pixels. Defaults to 32 px, converted to EMUs.
Optional horizontal offset in pixels from the cell's left boundary. Positive values move the image right; defaults to 0 px.
Optional vertical offset in pixels from the cell's top boundary. Positive values move the image down; defaults to 0 px.

member.summary: Enables a totals row for the table covering and assigns per-column functions by header name. Supported functions are those in TotalsRowFunctionValues (Sum, Average, Count, Min, Max, etc.).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.AddAutoFilter(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.UInt32,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String}})
member.summary: Adds an AutoFilter to the worksheet or table.
member.param: The cell range to apply the filter to.
member.remarks: Smart conflict resolution: - If a table exists on the same range, the AutoFilter is added to the table instead of the worksheet - If a worksheet-level AutoFilter exists, it's removed before adding to the table - This ensures Excel won't complain about conflicting filters Order doesn't matter - whether you add Table then AutoFilter or AutoFilter then Table, the final result will be a table with AutoFilter enabled.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.AddTable(System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.TableStyle)
member.summary: Adds an Excel table to the worksheet over the specified range.
member.param: Cell range (e.g. "A1:B3") defining the table area.
Indicates whether the first row is a header row.
Name of the table. If empty, a default name is used.
member.remarks: All cells within must exist. Missing cells are automatically created with empty values.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or empty.
Thrown when is not in a valid format.
Thrown when the specified range overlaps with an existing table.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.GetTableRange(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the A1 range covered by a table on this worksheet.
member.returns: The table reference, or null when no matching table exists.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.AddTable(System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.TableStyle,System.Boolean,OfficeIMO.Excel.TableNameValidationMode)
member.summary: Adds an Excel table to the worksheet over the specified range with optional AutoFilter and name validation behavior.
member.param: Cell range (e.g. "A1:B3") defining the table area.
Indicates whether the first row is a header row.

Name of the table. If empty, a default name is used. Examples: "My Table" becomes "My_Table"; "123Report" becomes "_123Report"; spaces and invalid characters are replaced with underscores. If a name already exists in this workbook, a numeric suffix is appended (e.g., "Table", "Table2").
Whether to include AutoFilter dropdowns in the table headers.
member.remarks: Smart AutoFilter handling: - If includeAutoFilter is true and a worksheet-level AutoFilter exists on the same range, it's moved to the table (preserving any filter criteria). - If includeAutoFilter is false and a worksheet-level AutoFilter exists, it's removed (Excel doesn't allow both worksheet AutoFilter and a table on the same range). - Order doesn't matter — the final state will be consistent regardless of operation order.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheet.EnsureValidUniqueTableName(System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.TableNameValidationMode)
member.summary: Ensures a valid and unique table name according to OfficeIMO rules. Rules: - Allowed characters: letters, digits, underscore; spaces become underscores. - Names cannot start with a digit; an underscore is prefixed if necessary. - Names are scoped per workbook and checked case-insensitively. - When is , throws for invalid input. Examples: - "My Table" ⇒ "My_Table" - "Sales#2025" ⇒ "Sales_2025" - "123Report" ⇒ "_123Report" - If "Table" already exists, next becomes "Table2" ("Table3", ...)

member.summary: Controls how heavy operations in OfficeIMO.Excel run (sequential vs parallel) based on workload size. Configure global and per‑operation thresholds and optionally observe decisions.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.Mode
member.summary: Global execution mode. When , the policy selects sequential or parallel per operation.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.ParallelThreshold
member.summary: Default threshold above which Automatic switches to Parallel.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.OperationThresholds
member.summary: Per-operation thresholds (names: "CellValues", "InsertObjects", "AutoFitColumns", ...).

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.MaxDegreeOfParallelism
member.summary: Optional cap for parallel compute phase.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.OnDecision
member.summary: Structured diagnostics (operation, items, decided mode).

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.OnTiming
member.summary: Optional timing callback invoked by long-running operations to report elapsed time. Provides a lightweight hook for performance monitoring in large workbooks.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.OnInfo
member.summary: Optional informational callback for verbose/debug diagnostics (no sheet output). Use to observe non-fatal events (e.g., grid overflow handled by Shrink/Summarize).

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.DiagnosticsRequested
member.summary: Indicates whether consumers explicitly requested diagnostics. When true, operations configured with will run validation even if no callbacks are wired.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.WorksheetValidation
member.summary: Controls when worksheet mutation validation is executed. Defaults to running only when diagnostics are requested to avoid penalizing hot paths.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.UseOpenXmlValidatorInDebug
member.summary: Enables invoking while debugging. This incurs a significant cost and is ignored when not compiling in DEBUG mode.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.ReportTiming(System.String,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Helper to invoke the timing callback if configured.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.Decide(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Decide execution mode for a given operation and workload size.
member.param: Approximate number of items to process.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionPolicy.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a policy with recommended default thresholds for common operations.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.NameValidationMode.Sanitize
member.summary: Adjust invalid input into a legal Excel defined name (replace invalid characters with underscore, ensure a valid starting character, trim length). Names that resemble A1/R1C1 cell addresses are prefixed with an underscore. Out-of-bounds A1 ranges are clamped to Excel limits.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.NameValidationMode.Strict
member.summary: Enforce Excel rules strictly: throws when the name is invalid or the A1 range exceeds Excel bounds.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheetReader.ReadRows(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lazily reads each row within the A1 range as a typed object array. Values are converted using shared strings and styles (date detection).
member.returns: Sequence of rows as object?[] with fixed width equal to the range width. Rows without any cells yield null.
member.remarks: A null row is emitted when the worksheet row is missing from the requested range or when it contains no cells within the specified bounds. Consumers that require dense data can call which throws an when an empty worksheet row is encountered.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelSheetReader.ReadRangeStream(System.String,System.Int32,System.Nullable{OfficeIMO.Excel.ExecutionMode},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lazily reads a rectangular A1 range as ordered row chunks. DOM traversal is single-threaded; per-chunk value conversion is offloaded in parallel based on Execution policy.

member.summary: Static helpers for quick one‑liner read operations. These helpers open the workbook read‑only, materialize the requested result, and then dispose it. Use these when you prefer brevity over streaming/iterator patterns.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadUsedRangeObjects(OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Reads the used range of a sheet as dictionaries (first row as headers) using an already-open ExcelDocument. Avoids re-opening the file and potential file locks.
member.param: Optional read options controlling type conversion and trimming.
member.returns: List of dictionaries representing each row in the sheet's used range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadRange(System.String,System.String,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Reads a rectangular A1 range (e.g., "A1:C10") from the specified sheet into a dense 2D array of typed values.
member.param: Optional read options (presets are available in ).
member.returns: Typed matrix with nulls for blank cells.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadRangeAsDataTable(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Reads an A1 range into a DataTable. When is true, the first row is used as column names.
member.param: Whether to treat the first row as column headers.
member.returns: DataTable containing values from the range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadRangeAs``1(System.String,System.String,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Reads an A1 range into a dense typed matrix.
member.typeparam: Element type for each cell in the matrix.
member.returns: Typed matrix populated from the requested range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadColumnAs``1(System.String,System.String,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)

member.returns: List of typed values.

member.summary: Reads an A1 range into a dense 2D array of typed values by sheet index (1‑based).
member.returns: Typed matrix with nulls for blank cells.

member.param: Whether to treat the first row as column headers.
member.returns: DataTable containing values from the range.

member.summary: Reads an A1 range into a dense typed matrix by sheet index (1‑based).
member.typeparam: Element type for each cell in the matrix.
member.returns: Typed matrix populated from the requested range.

member.summary: Reads the used range of a sheet as dictionaries (first row as headers).
member.param: Optional read options controlling type conversion and trimming.
member.returns: List of dictionaries representing each row in the sheet's used range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelRead.ReadRangeObjects(System.String,System.String,System.String,OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)

member.param: Inclusive A1 range (first row must contain headers).

member.returns: List of populated from each data row.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions
member.summary: Reading options controlling conversion behavior and execution policy.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.Execution
member.summary: Execution policy used to decide Sequential vs Parallel conversion. Reuses the writer-side policy for symmetry.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.UseCachedFormulaResult
member.summary: Use cached formula results when present; otherwise returns the formula string.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.TreatDatesUsingNumberFormat
member.summary: Interpret numeric cells with a date-like number format as DateTime (OADate).

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.Culture
member.summary: Culture used when parsing numbers and dates stored as strings.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.FillBlanksInRanges
member.summary: When true, matrix/range readers fill unspecified cells with nulls.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.NormalizeHeaders
member.summary: Normalize headers for object mapping by trimming and collapsing whitespace.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.NumericAsDecimal
member.summary: When true, numeric cells are returned as decimal where possible; otherwise double is used.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.StrictTypedMapping
member.summary: When true, typed object readers throw if selected headers cannot be mapped deterministically to writable properties.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.CellValueConverter
member.summary: Optional cell-level converter hook. If provided and it returns a handled value, the built-in conversion pipeline is skipped and the returned value is used.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.TypeConverter
member.summary: Optional type conversion hook used by typed readers (ReadColumnAs/ReadRowsAs/ReadRangeAs and object mapping). If it returns ok=true, its value is used; otherwise the built-in converter is used.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes reading defaults and per-operation thresholds.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1
member.summary: Utility helpers for parsing and converting Excel A1 references. Public so examples and consumers can reuse consistent logic without re-implementing regexes or math.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1.ParseCellRef(System.String)
member.summary: Parses a single A1 cell reference (e.g., "B5") into a 1-based (row, column) tuple. Returns (0,0) when the input does not match a valid simple cell reference.
member.param: A1 cell reference, without sheet prefix.
member.returns: Tuple of row and column (1-based). Returns (0,0) if invalid.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1.TryParseRange(System.String,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Tries to parse an A1 range (e.g., "A1:B10") into 1-based, normalized bounds. Returns false when the input is not a valid A1 range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1.ParseRange(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an A1 range (e.g., "A1:B10") into 1-based, normalized bounds. If the bounds are inverted, they are swapped so that r1 <= r2 and c1 <= c2.
member.param: A1 range string, without sheet prefix.
member.returns: (r1, c1, r2, c2) 1-based coordinates.
member.exception: Thrown when the input is not a valid A1 range.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1.ColumnLettersToIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Converts column letters (e.g., "A", "AA") to a 1-based column index. Non-letter characters are ignored; returns 0 for empty/invalid input.
member.returns: 1-based column index, or 0 when input yields no letters.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.A1.ColumnIndexToLetters(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a 1-based column index to Excel column letters (e.g., 1→"A", 27→"AA").
member.returns: Excel column letters; returns "A" for non-positive inputs.
member.example: string col = A1.ColumnIndexToLetters(28); // "AB" int idx = A1.ColumnLettersToIndex("AB"); // 28

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.CellValueInfo
member.summary: A typed cell value with row/column coordinates.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.CellValueInfo.Row
member.summary: One-based row index.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.CellValueInfo.Column
member.summary: One-based column index.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.CellValueInfo.Value
member.summary: Cell value after conversion.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.CellValueInfo.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Object)
member.summary: Creates a typed cell value with coordinates.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets
member.summary: Convenience presets for common read conversions to keep user code minimal. Provides several choices from minimal to more aggressive conversions.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets.None
member.summary: No extra converters. Equivalent to default ExcelReadOptions with only built-in behavior.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets.Simple
member.summary: Minimal, non-intrusive converters: - Y/Yes/N/No to bool - Currency-like strings to decimal (tries CurrentCulture, InvariantCulture, a few common locales)

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets.DecimalFirst
member.summary: Like Simple() but prefers decimal for numeric cells when possible.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets.ApplyBasicConverters(OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadOptions)
member.summary: Applies the same converters as returned by to an existing options instance.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelReadPresets.Aggressive
member.summary: Aggressive converters for convenience: - Extended bool: true/false/1/0/on/off/y/n/yes/no - Percent strings (e.g., 12%) to decimals (0.12) - Currency and plain numeric strings to decimal/double/int where appropriate - ISO date strings to DateTime

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.SharedStringPlanner
member.summary: Planner for SharedStrings to avoid DOM mutations during parallel compute. Collects distinct strings concurrently, applies them under document lock, and fixes up prepared cell values to reference shared string indices.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.SharedStringPlanner.ApplyTo(OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument)
member.summary: Apply collected strings to the document's SharedStringTable and build final index mapping. Must be called inside a serialized apply stage (under document write lock).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.SharedStringPlanner.Fixup(System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.CellValue,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.EnumValue{DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.CellValues}}@)
member.summary: Fixes a prepared cell tuple in-place, replacing SharedString text with its index.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.SharedStringPlanner.ApplyAndFixup(System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.CellValue,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.EnumValue{DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.CellValues}}[],OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelDocument)
member.summary: Applies planner to document and fixes all prepared cells. Must be called inside serialized apply stage.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.SheetNameValidationMode
member.summary: Controls how worksheet names are validated when adding sheets.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.SheetNameValidationMode.None
member.summary: Do not alter the provided name. The caller is responsible for ensuring it obeys Excel rules.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.SheetNameValidationMode.Sanitize
member.summary: Coerce the provided name into a valid Excel worksheet name by applying the rules: - Replace invalid characters (: \ / ? * [ ]) with underscore. - Trim leading/trailing apostrophes and whitespace. - Truncate to 31 characters. - Ensure non-empty; fall back to "Sheet". - Ensure uniqueness across the workbook by appending " (2)", " (3)", etc.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.SheetNameValidationMode.Strict
member.summary: Enforce Excel rules strictly; throw if the name is empty, too long (> 31), contains invalid characters (: \ / ? * [ ]), or would collide (case-insensitive) with an existing sheet.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.StylePlanner
member.summary: Minimal planner for number formats/styles. Collects distinct number format strings during compute, then creates NumberingFormats and CellFormats once in the apply stage.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.TableNameValidationMode
member.summary: Controls how AddTable validates the provided table name.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.TableNameValidationMode.Sanitize
member.summary: Replace invalid characters, normalize, and ensure uniqueness.

member.name: F:OfficeIMO.Excel.TableNameValidationMode.Strict
member.summary: Throw descriptive exceptions when the name doesn't meet requirements.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelColor.ToArgbHex(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Color)
member.summary: Converts SixLabors Color to ARGB hex string expected by Spreadsheet colors (AARRGGBB, uppercase).

member.summary: Returns the named sheet if it exists, otherwise creates it; falls back to the last sheet.

member.summary: Resolves a cell address from either A1 notation or row/column coordinates.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ExcelHostExtensions.ResolveColumnIndex(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a column index from either a numeric index or column letters.

Culture used for currency symbol; defaults to current culture.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions
member.summary: Configuration for flattening objects into key/value pairs where keys represent dotted paths.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.ExpandProperties
member.summary: Property names or dotted paths to expand (descend into) rather than treat as simple values.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.IncludeFullObjects
member.summary: When true, includes the original object under its path in addition to expanded fields.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.MaxDepth
member.summary: Maximum recursion depth when expanding nested objects.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.HeaderCase
member.summary: Header casing strategy for generated column names.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.HeaderPrefixTrimPaths
member.summary: Optional prefixes to trim from generated headers.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Columns
member.summary: Optional explicit column whitelist (dotted paths).

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Ignore
member.summary: Paths to exclude from output.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.IncludeProperties
member.summary: Optional convenience include list using property names or dotted paths. When specified, only matching columns are kept. A match occurs when the full dotted path matches or the last segment (property name) matches case-insensitively. This is a friendlier alias for .

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.ExcludeProperties
member.summary: Optional convenience exclude list using property names or dotted paths. A match occurs when the full dotted path matches or the last segment (property name) matches case-insensitively. This augments which treats values as dotted-path prefixes.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PinnedFirst
member.summary: Optional list of dotted paths to pin to the front of the generated column order when is not specified. Matching paths keep the specified order; any non-matching paths follow in natural order.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PinnedLast
member.summary: Optional list of dotted paths to pin to the end of the generated column order. Processed after .

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PropertyPriority
member.summary: Optional per-column priority ordering. Lower numbers appear earlier. Keys can be dotted paths or property names. When not specified, relative discovery order is preserved. Applied after and before .

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.NullPolicy
member.summary: How null values are represented.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.DefaultValues
member.summary: Per‑path default values used when is selected.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Formatters
member.summary: Per‑path formatting delegates applied to values.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.CollectionMode
member.summary: How to handle collections.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.CollectionJoinWith
member.summary: Delimiter used when is selected.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.CollectionMapColumns
member.summary: Maps collection paths (e.g., "ScoreBreakdown") to dynamic columns using element properties. Example: map path "ScoreBreakdown" with KeyProperty="Name", ValueProperty="Value" to produce columns like "ScoreBreakdown.HasMX" = 2, "ScoreBreakdown.EffectiveSPFSends" = 2.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PinFirst(System.String[])
member.summary: Adds or appends to while preserving order and uniqueness.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PinLast(System.String[])
member.summary: Adds or appends to while preserving order and uniqueness.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.PriorityOrder(System.String[])
member.summary: Sets priority order 1..N for the provided paths (full path or property name).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Include(System.String[])
member.summary: Appends to while preserving uniqueness.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Exclude(System.String[])
member.summary: Appends to while preserving uniqueness.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Excel.ObjectFlattenerOptions.Order(System.String[],System.String[],System.String[])
member.summary: One-call convenience: set pin-first, priority order, and pin-last. Any argument may be null; order is applied as PinFirst → PriorityOrder → PinLast.
